titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 575

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=892 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.50617+00 | translation moa=893 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.516476+00

div.p:
Dauid rex Scot’ . G . Ridell’ . vicecomiti de Rokesburg . salutem . Tibi precipio ut
terra monacorum Dunelm’ quam Gospatric de Dunbar dedit ...
decessu . ponatur in respectum donec in illam uenero prouinciam . Nemo
de monacis nec de Gospatrico aliquam operationem . nec seruitium capiet sed monaci teneant illam terram bene et in pace . et quiete infra suum
terminum. Et non permittas quod aliquis eis iniuriam faciat sed fac inde sicut meam dominiam faceres . t’ . hugone de Moruill'

p.cl: David, king of the Scots, to Gervase Ridel, sheriff of Roxburgh, greeting.

I enjoin you that the land of the monks of Durham which Cospatric of Dunbar gave [?upon his father's death] be placed in respite until I shall have come to that province. No-one shall demand of the monks nor of Cospatric any work nor service, but the monks shall hold that land well and in peace, and quit within their boundary. And you are not to allow anyone to do injustice but act thereupon just as you would do for my demesne.
